# Midterm 1 {.unnumbered .centered-title}

::: {.content-hidden}
{{< include ../_macros.qmd >}}
:::

::::: {.exam}

{{< include _exam-header.qmd >}}
{{< include _blank-page.qmd >}}

::: {#prb-coherence-identity .question points=10}
Recall from @def-hypersquare that the boundary of a 2-face is governed by transport operators. Prove that if the transport operators on the four edges of a 2-face are given by reflection matrices $R_\theta$, the face-dependent automorphism must be the identity $\Id$.
:::

{{< include _blank-page.qmd >}}

:::: {#prb-discrete-action .question}
Consider a particle moving along a hypersquare whose discrete action is modeled by the polynomial $A(t) = t^3 - 3t^2 + 2$. 

::: {#prb-action-deriv .part points=3}
Compute the formal derivative $A'(t)$ with respect to the phase parameter $t$.
:::

\vfill

::: {#prb-critical-phases .part points=3}
Find the critical phases $t$ where the action is locally minimized or maximized.
:::

\vfill

::: {#prb-self-intersections .part points=4}
Sketch the action curve on the provided 2-face grid, clearly labeling the anomalous self-intersections.
:::

\vfill

::::

{{< include _blank-page.qmd >}}

::: {#prb-maximum-discrete-action .question points=10}
As you proved in @prb-coherence-identity, sequential reflections on a 2-face yield the identity automorphism, effectively trapping the particle. Suppose the accumulated discrete action $\mathcal{S}(t)$ under the transport holonomy $\mathcal{H}_\gamma(t)$ is governed by the trace of the hypersquare connection form:
$$
\mathcal{S}(t) = \frac{1}{2\pi i} \oint_{\dlp{Q}} \Trace \left( \mathcal{H}_\gamma(t)^{-1} \nabla_{\!t} \mathcal{H}_\gamma(t) \right) \dl{\tau}
$$
Assuming that for our specific hypersquare, this contour integral over the boundary $\dlp{Q}$ simplifies exactly to the polynomial action $A(t)$ evaluated at phase $t$, use the critical phases of @prb-action-deriv to find the maximum discrete action the particle can accumulate before the holonomy forces a periodic phase shift.
:::

{{< include _blank-page.qmd >}}

:::::
